channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Police foil kidnap attempt, arrest 66-year-old suspect in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Anambra State Police Command foils a kidnap attempt, rescues a victim, and arrests a 66-year-old suspect involved in a syndicate defrauding residents.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-foil-kidnap-attempt-arrest-66-year-old-suspect-in-anambra/

item.title: Nigeria, Egypt partner to fight drug cartels, sign anti-narcotics MoU
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria and Egypt are set to sign an MoU to strengthen cooperation against drug cartels, enhancing intelligence sharing and joint operations to combat illi
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigeria-egypt-partner-to-fight-drug-cartels-sign-anti-narcotics-mou/

item.title: Gunmen kill Lagos NURTW organising secretary
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos NURTW Organising Secretary, Toba Ajiboye, was killed by gunmen. Learn more about the tragic death of the union official and reactions from NURTW chai
Read More: https://punchng.com/gunmen-kill-lagos-nurtw-organising-secretary/

item.title: Tinubu promises clean water supply to all FCT area councils
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu assures all FCT area councils will receive clean water supply, extending the Bwari Water Supply Network to improve public health and digni
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-promises-clean-water-supply-to-all-fct-area-councils/

item.title: Police confirm death of Kwara farmer, begin probe
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kwara State Police confirm the killing of farmer Azeez Garba in Isin LGA and launch a comprehensive probe. Family demands justice for the deceased.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-confirm-death-of-kwara-farmer-begin-probe/

item.title: Efforts ongoing to ensure implementation of approved salary increase – DHQ
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Defence Headquarters reaffirms commitment to improving military veterans welfare, promising better healthcare, prompt pensions, and enhanced packages.
Read More: https://punchng.com/efforts-ongoing-to-ensure-implementation-of-approved-salary-increase-dhq/

item.title: Why we rescinded state police bill for Tinubu’s proposal — Reps
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives rescinded its state police bill to consider President Tinubu’s executive proposal, aiming for a coherent constitutional framew
Read More: https://punchng.com/why-we-rescinded-state-police-bill-for-tinubus-proposal-reps/

item.title: 2027: ADP presidential candidate picks running mate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Action Democratic Party’s presidential candidate, Ambassador Aliyu Bin Abbas, names Hon. Chinazam Ike as his running mate for the 2027 election.
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-adp-presidential-candidate-picks-running-mate/

item.title: UPDATED: Court orders arrest of ‘Fake’ agency DG, Adeniyi
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Federal High Court orders the arrest of Adeniyi Adeyemi, self-acclaimed DG of a ‘fake’ presidential agency, for alleged forgery and impersonation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/breaking-court-orders-arrest-of-fake-agency-dg-adeniyi/

item.title: VIDEO: We’re a generation of jokers, not builders — Flutterwave co-founder
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Flutterwave co-founder Iyinoluwa Aboyeji criticises Nigerian youth, calling them a “generation of jokers” focused on performance over building lasting inst
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-were-a-generation-of-jokers-not-builders-flutterwave-co-founder/

item.title: Jamie Dimon says ‘booming’ markets and a ‘fine’ consumer fueled JPMorgan’s blowout profits
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: JPMorgan blew past earnings expectations for the second quarter, as CEO Jamie Dimon said markets are booming.

item.title: Kwara APC Reconciliation Committee Extends Olive Branch To G15
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Kwara State All Progressives Congress (APC) Reconciliation Committee has intensified efforts to resolve the party’s internal disputes. The committee therefore urged members of the party to halt inflammatory public exchanges while extending an invitation to the G15 Group to join ongo

item.title: ‘Hand Of God,’ Falkland Crisis: England, Argentina Seek National Pride
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: England and Argentina will meet in the semifinals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup at Atlanta Stadium on Wednesday night, reigniting a rivalry forged in the “Hand of God” and shadowed by the Falklands crisis. Both nations chase a measure of national pride on football’s biggest stage, bea

item.title: Nigerian woman who moved to UK on skilled worker visa dies by suicide
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A coroner in the United Kingdom has ruled that a 27-year-old Nigerian woman, Beatrice Solomon, died by suicide after an inquest into her death. The post Nigerian woman who moved to UK on skilled worker visa dies by suicide appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Nigeria will be peaceful, no more deaths – Mrs Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The wife of President Bola Tinubu, Oluremi, on Tuesday declared that Nigeria will be a peaceful country that is blessed and fruitful. Mrs Tinubu also declared that Nigeria will not be a barren land but a fruitful country. The First Lady disclosed this during the commissioning of water supply network

item.title: Benue: One killed as suspected herdsmen attack another Otukpo community
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The latest attack came less than 48 hours after the deadly invasion of Otukpo-Nobi community in the same LGA, where more than 18 persons were reportedly killed, raising fresh concerns over the worsening security situation in Benue South. The post Benue: One killed as suspected herdsmen attack anothe

item.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.

item.title: AFCON 2025: What is the latest update on Senegal's appeal to CAS?
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: The reports circulating on social media claiming that the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) has overturned CAF's decision and awarded the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title to Senegal are false. As of now, CAS has not issued any final ruling on the appeal filed by the Senegalese Football Federation

item.title: ‘I will continue to criticise Ebonyi govt where necessary’ – PDP guber candidate
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The 2027 governorship candidate of the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP in Ebonyi, Chief Ifeanyi-Chukwuma Odii, has dismissed allegations by the member representing Ikwo/Ezza South Federal Constituency, Mr Chinedu Ogah, that he has been sponsoring cyberbullying campaigns against Ebonyi government. Odii

item.title: Senate urges FG to intensify military operations against terrorists, bandits
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Senate has called on the Federal Government to sustain and intensify ongoing military and intelligence operations against terrorists, kidnappers, bandits and other criminal elements across the country. The post Senate urges FG to intensify military operations against terrorists, bandits appeared

item.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from donations for first time in 20 years
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Billionaire investor Warren Buffett has excluded the Gates Foundation from his annual midyear charitable donations for the first time since 2006, thus redirecting nearly $6 billion worth of Berkshire Hathaway shares to four philanthropic organisations run by his children. The post Warren Buffett exc

item.title: Marvel Opens Early Ticket Sales for Avengers: Doomsday, Confirms Epic Runtime Ahead of Release
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Marvel Studios is giving fans an early chance to secure seats for ‘Avengers: Doomsday’, with premium-format tickets set to go on sale months before the blockbuster arrives in cinemas. Disney is expected to begin selling domestic tickets for specially designated Infinity Vision screenings on July 20,

item.title: Iran abused mobile networks’ vulnerabilities to locate U.S. military in the Middle East, report says
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The Iranian government exploited well-known flaws in cellphone networks to locate and then strike U.S. military personnel in the build-up and beginning of the war.

item.title: NDC backs Makinde’s call for UN probe into Oriire school abduction
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde’s call for an independent United Nations (UN) investigation into the abduction of students and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of the state has been endorsed by the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC). The party said a transparent and impartial international in

item.title: State Police: Reps reverse self, pass executive bill through second reading
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The House of Representatives on Tuesday reversed its earlier resolution on the passage of the bill seeking to establish State Police as part of the The post State Police: Reps reverse self, pass executive bill through second reading appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: NiDCOM, NIMC Partner to Strengthen Digital Identity System for Nigerians in Diaspora
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Nigerians in Diaspora Commission (NiDCOM) and the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) have commenced collaboration aimed at strengthening digital identity, digital payment systems and trusted data exchange for Nigerians at home and abroad. The partnership was announced after a strateg

item.title: Lagos shifts 2026 JSS placement test over monthly sanitation exercise
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Lagos State Government has rescheduled the 2026 Placement Test for admission into public junior secondary schools from Saturday, July 25, to Tuesday, July 28, after the examination date coincided with the state's monthly environmental sanitation exercise. The post Lagos shifts 2026 JSS placement

item.title: Warsh Says Fed Has ‘No Tolerance’ for Elevated Inflation
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh gives his opening remarks before questioning from members of the House Financial Services Committee. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.description: Pastor Enoch Adeboye, General Overseer of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG), has penned a touching birthday message to his wife, Pastor Folu Adeboye, as she marked her 78th birthday. In a heartfelt tribute, the revered cleric reflected on their 58-year marriage, expressing gratitude for he

item.title: Countdown to 14th Women AFCON: Super Falcons to battle Black Queens in friendly
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: As part of their build-up to the 14th Women Africa Cup of Nations finals which begin in Morocco on Sunday, Cup-holders Nigeria will do battle with Ghana in a friendly match scheduled for Casablanca on Saturday.

item.title: The Canada PGWP trap: Why Nigerian grads are getting deported after 3 years
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: For many Nigerian students, graduating from a Canadian institution and securing a Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) feels like the final step toward building a long-term future abroad. But for a growing number of graduates, the reality is far different. The post The Canada PGWP trap: Why Nigerian

item.description: The Federal Government has reaffirmed its commitment to delivering 100,000 affordable housing units nationwide under the Renewed Hope Cities and Estates Programme through outright purchase, mortgage financing, and rent-to-own schemes targeted at low- and middle-income Nigerians. item.title: Sarah Martins Fires Back at Troll Who Said She Looks Older After Alleged Surgery
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nollywood actress Sarah Martins has fired back at a social media troll who claimed she looks older than her age because of alleged cosmetic surgery. The troll alleged that although she is 10 years older than the actress, Martins now looks old enough to be her aunt. She also claimed she had previousl

item.title: Dangote Refinery: Some depot owners increase petrol price after hike crude cost
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Depot owners have raised their premium motor spirit ex-depot prices following rising crude oil prices and the Dangote Refinery’s resumption of refined product sales in dollars. Checks by DAILY POST showed that several depot owners such as Sahara, Integrated, African Terminal, Emedab, and Ranoi

item.title: Oyedele Seeks Stronger Regional Cooperation To Boost Tax Revenue
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Nigeria’s Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy, Taiwo Oyedele, has called for closer collaboration among West African nations to strengthen tax administration, boost compliance with regional tax policies, and increase domestic revenue generation across the ECOWAS region

item.title: Brighton Land Vuskovic In Club Record £46m Deal
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Brighton have completed the club-record signing of Croatia defender Luka Vuskovic from Tottenham for £46m, with the fee potentially rising to £50m through add-ons. The 19-year-old, who joined Spurs in 2025 from Hajduk Split, has signed a five-year deal with an option for a further year after Brighto

item.title: A’Ibom Rebuilding Tourism For Global Competitiveness, Says Udoh

item.title: Credibility Of Elections More Critical Than Number Of Parties On Ballot – Hakeem Baba-Ahmed
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Former Special Adviser on Political Matters to the Vice President, Dr. Hakeem Baba-Ahmed, has said the credibility of the 2027 general elections is more important than the number of political parties that eventually field candidates. Speaking in an exclusive interview, Baba-Ahmed was asked what woul

item.title: Global sustainability summit enters 5th year as delegates commit to sustainability agenda
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Delegates from more than 37 countries have reaffirmed their commitment to advancing the global sustainability agenda as the Global Sustainability Summit & Awards marked its fifth edition at the House of Lords in London. The summit, convened by Ambassador Canon Otto, FRSA, brought together world

item.title: Why Stellar Bank Earnings Aren't Enough
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Baird Senior Research Analyst David George joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why stellar bank earnings aren't translating into stock gains. He says investors are facing a "buy the rumor, sell the news" moment as expectations reached extreme levels, while emphasizing that banks are far safer

item.title: Fatal ICE Shooting Draws Maine Vigils as Scrutiny Grows
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Security footage obtained by The Associated Press shows the aftermath of the shooting of a man in Maine by ICE officers, including a white car circling in an intersection, a law enforcement SUV stopping the car and agents opening the door and pulling out a body. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: 26-year-old shot and killed by ICE agent in Maine sparks demonstrations
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US ICE agents shot and killed a 26-year-old Colombian man in Maine early Monday morning. Department of Homeland Security secretary Markwayne Mullin first said that the man was a target of an ICE investigation and that he had attempted to "weaponise" his vehicle. Later the story changed – it was conf

item.title: France holds largest military parade ever in large show of unity for Ukraine
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Every year, France holds a military parade to celebrate its national holiday and the anniversary of the storming of the Bastille fortress during the French revolution. But this year’s July 14th parade was the largest ever – President Emmanuel Macron had rallied European allies behind Ukraine for his

item.title: FCT Projects: We focus on results, not excuses
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has said his administration is committed to delivering results for Nigerians and does not believe in making excuses.

item.title: Bangkok bar fire death toll rises to 30, 24 people remain in critical condition
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 30 people have now been confirmed dead in a blaze that engulfed a bar in northern Bangkok in the early hours of Monday morning. 27 victims have been formally identified, with authorities still working to confirm the identities of the remaining three. Meanwhile, forensic investigators are se

item.title: Oriire Abduction: Senate condemns Makinde over UN invitation, commends Tinubu, security agencies on rescue

item.description: The lawmakers interpreted governor Makinde’s statement as capable of diminishing the federal government’s efforts to address insecurity and also undermine Nigeria’s sovereignty. item.title: Anambra Police Foil Kidnap Plot, Arrest Alleged Fraud Syndicate Leader
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Anambra State Police Command has foiled an alleged plot by a gang of gunmen to kidnap some prominent personalities in the state. The command also arrested the alleged leader of a three-man syndicate notorious for defrauding unsuspecting members of the public in Anambra and Enugu states. In a sta

item.title: Warsh Says Fed Has 'No Tolerance' for Persistent Inflation
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said policymakers at the central bank have no tolerance for high inflation during remarks to the House Financial Services Committee. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Aboard French fighter jets defending European borders from Russia
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: As Europe decides how to handle Russian aggression, French Rafale fighter jets are on the front lines. Our colleagues at France 2 headed to Lithuania to film French pilots defending Europe's borders. Constantly patrolling, the French crews deter Russian drones- and sometimes even planes – who enter

item.title: I’m de-influencing you from buying the RingConn 3 (even though it’s pretty)
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The RingConn 3 actually looks like real jewelry, not a wearable -- but its fitness tracking and headache detection features are disappointing.

item.title: Senate rejects Makinde’s call for UN probe into Oyo school abduction
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Senators disagreed with Mr Makinde's proposal, maintaining that involving the UN in the matter could undermine Nigeria's sovereignty and the efforts of local security agencies. item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has issued a bench warrant for the arrest of the self-styled Director-General of an alleged “non-existent” Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council, PFIPC, Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi. Justice Mohammed Umar issued the arrest order following an oral application mad

item.title: Court Acquits Man Accused Of Stabbing Friend To death
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: An Abuja High Court on Tuesday acquitted a 29-year-old man, Simanyi Lawal, of stabbing his casual friend, Farouk Hamza, to death. Delivering judgment, Justice Cyprian Agashieze held that the prosecution did not present eyewitnesses before the court to prove that the stabbing occurred. “The pro

item.title: US CPI Falls for the First Time Since 2020, Core Gauge Flat
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US consumer prices declined in June for the first time in six years and a key gauge of underlying inflation was little changed, taking some pressure off the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates. Frances Donald, Chief Economist at RBC Capital Markets, reacts to June CPI. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: State police: Tinubu seeks constitutional approval following amendment
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has asked the House of Representatives to approve a constitutional amendment that would pave the way for the establishment of state police. The post State police: Tinubu seeks constitutional approval following amendment appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Bayelsa Police Arrest 10 Cult Suspects After Deadly Attack
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Bayelsa State Police Command has arrested 10 suspected cultists in connection with a deadly attack in the Obele area of Ovom, Yenagoa, which claimed the life of a street-corner metal scavenger, popularly known as an “Iron Condemned” boy, and left three others injured. The Commissione

item.title: Court declares alleged fake PFIPC DG wanted, orders Adeyemi’s arrest
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Relying on Section 394 of the Administration of Criminal Justice Act (ACJA), the prosecution urged the court to compel the defendant's appearance through a warrant of arrest.

item.title: 7 less complicated ways to keep weevils, pests out of your stored beans
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Beans are a foundational staple in millions of households, serving as an affordable and highly nutritious source of plant-based protein. When weevils invade the pantry, though, it seems like bean quality may be a battle that you're losing. The post 7 less complicated ways to keep weevils, pests out

item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: Real Madrid have reportedly taken the ongoing Negreira case to UEFA, formally requesting that European football's governing body strip Barcelona of the titles they won during the period the Catalan club made payments to former referees' committee vice-president José María Enríquez Negreira. There ar

item.title: Police recover corpse after bandits attack funeral convoy on Kogi highway
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Kogi State Police Command has confirmed that a corpse being transported from Lokoja to Benue State for burial was recovered The post Police recover corpse after bandits attack funeral convoy on Kogi highway appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes for the third day
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: A serious escalation is occurring in the fighting between the US and Iran. Overnight and into this morning, the American military targeted sites across the Islamic Republic – while Iran fired ballistic missiles at US airbases in Jordan and Bahrain. There has been an escalation in the war of words to

item.title: France prepares for World Cup semifinal against Spain
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: In Dallas, football fans are gearing up for one of the World Cup's most keenly anticipated fixtures. The semifinal between France and Spain will see two of the tournament's best teams clash on Tuesday, with French stars such as Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé contending with a Spanish defence that

item.title: Oyo Abduction: Senate blasts Makinde for demanding UN probe
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigerian Senate has lambasted the Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, for calling for a United Nations-led probe into the abduction of pupils and teachers from Oriire Local Government Area of the state. Speaking at plenary on Tuesday, the Senate asked the governor to stop trivialising a very serio

item.title: Troops rescue seven kidnap victims along Riyom highway in Plateau
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The seven passengers kidnapped along the Riyom Highway in Plateau State on Monday have been rescued by troops of Operation Enduring Peace, The post Troops rescue seven kidnap victims along Riyom highway in Plateau appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Rivers Assembly Threatens To Sanction Oil Firm For Shunning Invitations
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Rivers State House of Assembly has threatened to use its constitutional powers against Heirs Energies Limited over its alleged refusal to honour legislative invitations following a petition filed by the Omuohia Community in Igwuruta Town, in Ikwerre Local Government Area. The lawmakers frowned o

item.title: NIPSS DG Apologises To Gbong Gwom Jos, Berom Nation Over Controversial Remarks On Arise TV
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Director-General of the National Institute for Policy and Strategic Studies (NIPSS), Prof. Ayo Omotayo, has formally apologised to the Gbong Gwom Jos and Chairman of the Plateau State Council of Chiefs and Emirs, Da Jacob Gyang Buba, and the Berom Nation and the people of Plateau State over rema

item.title: India summons Iranian diplomat over missile killing of seafarer
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: India summoned Iran's deputy ambassador after an Indian crew member was killed in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Houthis target Saudi airport in retaliation for Sanaa air strike
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Yemen’s Houthi movement launched a series of ballistic missiles and drones at Saudi Arabia‘s Abha International Airport on Monday, marking the most significant escalation in the Yemen conflict since an informal truce took effect four years ago. The Houthis said the attack was carried out

item.title: SK Hynix Early ADR Options Trading Leans to Short-Term Contracts
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Options on SK Hynix Inc.’s American Depository Receipts began trading on US options exchanges Tuesday, giving traders in the world’s largest derivatives market easier access to the volatile South Korean memory chipmaker.

item.title: EPL: Alonso explains decision to join Chelsea instead of Liverpool
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso has explained why he chose to take charge of Chelsea instead of returning to Liverpool, insisting that timing was the key factor behind his decision.

item.title: Super Eagles Defender Agrees Personal Terms With Fulham
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Premier League club Fulham have reached an agreement on personal terms with Super Eagles defender Chibuike Nwaiwu as they step up their pursuit of the highly-rated centre-back. The Cottagers have made Nwaiwu one of their priority defensive targets this summer and remain determined to secure his sign

item.title: Transfer: Karl Darlow’s shirt number at Man Utd confirmed
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: New Manchester United signing, Karl Darlow’s shirt number at Old Trafford has been confirmed. Man United on Tuesday confirmed the signing of Darlow from Leeds United. The 35-year-old goalkeeper will join manager Michael Carrick’s first-team squad for the new season. In a post via the Red

item.title: Olympic funding questioned by nine EU nations over Russia readmission
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Nine EU nations urge funding cuts for sports bodies including International Olympic Committee over readmitting Russians.

item.title: Super Eagles Forward Tolu Arokodare Rejects Trabzonspor Move
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Super Eagles striker Tolu Arokodare has reportedly ruled out a potential transfer to Turkish Super Lig club Trabzonspor as he weighs his options ahead of the new season. The Wolverhampton Wanderers forward is expected to leave Molineux this summer following the club’s relegation from the Premier Lea

item.title: DMO Offers N1.2trn FGN Bonds For July Auction
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Debt Management Office (DMO), on behalf of the Federal Government of Nigeria, has announced a N1.2 trillion Federal Government of Nigeria (FGN) bond auction scheduled for July 20, 2026, as part of efforts to raise funds from the domestic debt market. According to the offer circular released by t
